one_line_thesis: Akamai is a profitable edge platform (Compute >50% of revenue) trading at ~19× FY2025 free cash flow; synthesis 15.83% supports hold while Q1 2026 margin compression warrants monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Akamai is a profitable edge platform pivoting from legacy content delivery toward cloud compute and security. FY2025 free cash flow was $1.01 billion ($6.88 per diluted share) after $508 million capital spending; management repurchased $800 million of stock. Q1 2026 revenue rose 5.8% but operating profit fell 26% as cost of revenue outpaced sales. Remaining performance obligation is ~$5.2 billion, supporting contracted revenue visibility. At $129.52, total synthesis implies 15.83% per year over seven years. Lawrence floor on filing-only base case is nearer 13%. Moat is stable on network sca...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Net income moved -10.5% versus the prior period (504,918.0 to 452,031.0).
Annual meeting: directors re-elected; stockholders approved special-meeting right (25% threshold) and 8M additional shares under incentive plan
Q1 2026 results: revenue $1.07B (+5.8% YoY); operating income $114M (-26% YoY) on higher cost of revenue and compute investment
FY2025 10-K: revenue $4.21B (+5.4%); net income $452M; free cash flow ~$1.01B; $800M share repurchases
Compute segment surpasses half of revenue — Linode and cloud/GPU infrastructure now largest solution line ($2.24B FY2025)
Accelerated share repurchases ($557M FY2024) while funding compute capex and operating-lease network expansion
Closed Linode acquisition — entry into cloud infrastructure/IaaS, foundation for Compute segment
Security solutions scaled past legacy delivery CDN — recurring security revenue becomes core mix
Tom Leighton (co-founder) CEO — long-tenured leadership through CDN-to-cloud transition
Akamai founded — distributed content delivery network pioneer

industry.structure: Edge CDN and web security are oligopolistic at scale (Akamai, Cloudflare, Fastly); cloud compute/IaaS is more fragmented with hyperscaler price competition. Akamai bundles delivery, security, and compute on a global edge network.
industry.share_shift: Akamai is gaining share in cloud compute and GPU-adjacent infrastructure while legacy delivery traffic mix shrinks; security revenue is flat to down year-over-year as competition intensifies.
industry.trend: AI inference, zero-trust security, and distributed cloud are pulling workloads to the edge; operating-lease data-center commitments and capex are rising to support compute growth.

risk_claims.claim: Net income moved -10.5% versus the prior period (504,918.0 to 452,031.0).
*Primary risk: Compute growth fails to offset Security pricing pressure, leaving consolidated free cash flow flat while lease obligations (~$1.6B ROU assets) and capex remain elevated.
Inversion: If Security stabilizes and Compute reaches 10%+ growth with margin recovery, multiple re-rates toward 25× cash flow.
